RNA-seq Differential Expression and WGCNA Report

DESeq2, enrichment, module preservation, and hub-gene summary

1 Executive Summary

This report summarizes the RNA-seq analysis pipeline for Breast invasive carcinoma (BRCA). The analysis compares tumor samples against normal samples, then uses WGCNA to find co-expression modules, annotate those modules, test preservation, and nominate hub-gene candidates.

In simple terms, the report follows this flow:

  1. Differential expression asks which genes change between tumor and normal.
  2. Enrichment asks what biological processes those changed genes represent.
  3. WGCNA groups genes into modules that move together across samples.
  4. Module annotation gives each module a biological label.
  5. Preservation checks whether tumor modules are also present in normal tissue.
  6. Hub-gene ranking finds central genes inside important modules.
Metric Value
Samples 1207
Genes tested for DE 18158
Significant DE genes 4823
WGCNA genes assigned to modules 5000
Non-grey WGCNA modules 12
Modules with annotation rows 12
Modules with preservation calls 14
Ranked hub rows 256
Priority hub rows 188

2 Differential Expression

The DESeq2 step estimates how much each gene changes between tumor and normal. log2FoldChange is the size of the change; positive values mean higher in tumor, and negative values mean lower in tumor. padj is the multiple-testing corrected p-value.

Direction Genes
Up-regulated 2854
Down-regulated 1969

3 Functional Enrichment

Over-representation analysis tests the significant up- and down-regulated gene lists. GSEA uses the full ranked gene list, so it can detect biology spread across many genes even when individual genes do not pass a hard cutoff.

Result Rows
GO ORA rows 1231
KEGG ORA rows 45
GO GSEA rows 792
KEGG GSEA rows 58

4 WGCNA Co-expression Network

WGCNA groups genes into modules based on similar expression patterns. A module is a color label, not a biological label by itself. The biological meaning comes from module-trait correlations and enrichment results.

Largest WGCNA modules
Module Genes
turquoise 1768
grey 727
brown 708
salmon 437
blue 436
red 230
black 221
pink 115
magenta 89
purple 80
greenyellow 75
tan 65
cyan 49

5 Module Annotation

This section translates color modules into biological themes. The best_padj column is the strongest adjusted p-value among the top GO terms for that module.

Module annotation summary
module size top_term_1 top_term_2 top_term_3 best_padj

black 218 vasculature development blood vessel development angiogenesis 3.95e-12

6 Module Preservation

Module preservation compares the tumor network against normal samples. A high Z-summary means the module structure is preserved in normal tissue. A low Z-summary suggests tumor-specific network rewiring.

Module preservation calls
module moduleSize Zsummary medianRank class
brown 708 59.08 NA preserved
blue 436 37.39 NA preserved
salmon 437 35.22 NA preserved
black 221 26.56 NA preserved
pink 115 24.73 NA preserved
cyan 49 24.45 NA preserved
purple 80 23.27 NA preserved
tan 65 22.16 NA preserved
red 230 20.00 NA preserved
turquoise 1768 17.52 NA preserved
magenta 89 14.34 NA preserved
greenyellow 75 8.52 NA preserved
all_modules 5000 4.48 NA moderate
grey 727 -0.43 NA not_preserved

7 Hub-gene Candidates

Hub genes are genes with high module membership (kME) inside their own module. The priority table keeps modules that are trait-associated, not preserved, or strongly enriched.

Module Priority reason Hub rows

7.1 Greenyellow Module Spotlight

The greenyellow module is preserved, but it is still biologically important because it has strong synaptic/neurotransmitter enrichment. In the hub-priority table this appears as priority_reason = enriched.

Greenyellow priority hub genes
gene_symbol kME log2FoldChange padj is_DE preservation_class enrichment_best_padj priority_reason
